title: Ceriscoides turgida

The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, and its extracts have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ceriscoides turgida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and xanthones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ceriscoides turgida is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the production of capsules, tablets, and teas to promote health and well-being.
taxonomy: Ceriscoides turgida is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is native to parts of Asia and is classified as a member of the genus Ceriscoides. The genus Ceriscoides is part of the tribe Spermaeae, which is further classified within the subfamily Cinchonoideae. The tribe Spermaeae is within the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. Ceriscoides turgida is often placed in the family Rubiaceae based on its morphological characteristics.
